Regulator_Type.reason: The paper explicitly mentions WRI1 as one of some transcription factors controlling oil biosynthesis.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper does not state that WRI1 belongs to the AP2/EREBP family.

Metabolic_Process_Controlled.reason: The text explicitly says WRI1 controls oil biosynthesis.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: WRI1 is mentioned only as a broad oil-biosynthesis regulator, not directly linked to palmitoleate production.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: The paper supports broad oil-biosynthesis control, but not a demonstrated effect on palmitoleate accumulation specifically.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: The paper states WRI1 controls oil biosynthesis, that regulation occurs at multiple levels, and that mechanistic details for 16:1 synthesis/regulation are not well understood.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: No direct experimental validation of WRI1 is presented in this review; it only cites prior studies.

Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product.reason: This wording properly states WRI1 is a broad upstream controller and that palmitoleate-specific control is not established.

Other_Important_Info.reason: It is accurate that WRI1 is the only regulator explicitly named and that its relation to palmitoleate is indirect/general.

corrections.reason: The paper does not state that WRI1 belongs to the AP2/EREBP family.

corrections.old_value: review citation of prior functional studies
corrections.reason: No direct experimental validation of WRI1 is presented in this review; it only cites prior studies.
